findings.title: Single point of failure: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/bubbletea/tea.go::(*Program).Run:972` dominates 31 functions (5%)
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/bubbletea/tea.go::(*Program).Run:972` dominates 31 of 618 functions (5.0%). PageRank percentile: 98%. Blast radius boundary: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/bubbletea/cursed_renderer.go::reset:590, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/bubbletea/environ.go::(EnvMsg).Getenv:24, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/bubbletea/environ.go::(EnvMsg).LookupEnv:32, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/bubbletea/tea.go::(*Program).RestoreTerminal:1325, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/bubbletea/tea.go::(*Program).Send:1164 (+8 more)
findings.suggested_fix: Consider splitting this function into smaller, independently callable units. Extract sub-functionality behind interfaces so callers have alternative paths.
findings.why_it_matters: A single point of failure means all dependent code paths funnel through one function. If this function has a bug, performance issue, or API change, the blast radius is disproportionately large.

findings.title: Long method: flush (317 lines)
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

8 levels of nesting (threshold: 7).

**Analysis:**

Entry point/handler (reduced severity)

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 2 functions in call cycle (complexity 20)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 2 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/bubbletea/tea.go::(*Program).execBatchMsg:899 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/bubbletea/tea.go::(*Program).execSequenceMsg:873. Aggregate complexity: 20.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider refactoring to eliminate direct mutual calls. Common strategies: merge the two functions, use a callback parameter, or introduce a shared data structure that both functions operate on.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Long method: Run (183 lines)
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Function 'Run' has 183 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
Orchestrator pattern: calls 21 functions (reduced severity)
findings.suggested_fix:
This appears to be an orchestrator function (coordinates many calls).
If it must remain long, ensure it:
1. Has clear section comments
2. Handles errors at each step
3. Has a clear flow (consider a state machine for complex flows)
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.
